DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT CHRISTINE O'REILLY VARON'S MOTION TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY CONTINUED FROM DEC-30-2004 TO DISCOVERY AT JAN-06-2005, 10:30 AM IN DEPT. 610. (610/CHAN)

DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT VARON'S MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY -- ANDREW S. WERNER APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY CHRISTINE VARON. DAVID J. MATTINGLY APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF SHANNON ABRAHAM. DAVID PARKER APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT/JOINDER PACIFIC UNION. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT VARON'S MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY AND DEFENDANT PACIFIC UNION REAL ESTATE'S JOINDER IN MOTION TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY ARE GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 2024 (E) THAT: THE DISCOVERY IS NECESSARY AS THE PREVIOUS DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF WAS CONDUCTED BEFORE DEFENDANTS WERE PARTIES TO THIS ACTION, THE DEFENDANTS PREVIOUSLY NOTICED THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF AND THE PARTIES AGREED TO A SPECIFIED DATE WHICH WAS SUBSEQUENTLY CANCELLED IN LIGHT OF THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, AND THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF WILL NEITHER DELAY THE TRIAL CURRENTLY SET FOR MARCH 7, 2005 NOR RESULT IN ANY PREJUDICE TO THE PARTIES. DISCOVERY IS RE-OPENED FOR THE LIMITED PURPOSE OF CONDUCTING THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE NOTICE OF DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS THAT WAS SERVED ON 12/28/01. THE DEPOSITION IS TO BE SCHEDULED AT A TIME MUTUALLY CONVENIENT TO THE PARTIES AND SHALL BE COMPLETED BY FEBRUARY 4, 2005. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT VARON'S MOTION TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY AND DEFENDANT PACIFIC UNION REAL STATE'S JOINDER IN MOTION TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY
